001/*
002 * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more
003 * contributor license agreements.  See the NOTICE file distributed with
004 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.
005 * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0
006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with
007 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
008 *
009 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
010 *
011 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
013 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
014 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.marshall.json;
018
019import java.io.*;
020import java.lang.reflect.*;
021
022import org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.*;
023import org.apache.juneau.marshall.*;
024import org.apache.juneau.marshall.parser.*;
025import org.apache.juneau.marshall.stream.*;
026
027/**
028 * Reference implementation of the public {@link TokenReader} surface for the JSON format.
029 *
030 * <p>
031 * Backed by the existing internal {@link ParserReader} plumbing.  Operates as a hand-rolled state
032 * machine over the JSON grammar &mdash; structural tokens (<c>{</c>, <c>}</c>, <c>[</c>, <c>]</c>,
033 * <c>,</c>, <c>:</c>) and the four scalar shapes (string, number, boolean, null).
034 *
035 * <p>
036 * Pipe-level settings (charset, auto-close, unbuffered) are configured on the
037 * {@link ParserPipe} the caller hands in.  Cursor-level behavior is driven by a small
038 * {@link Settings} bundle &mdash; currently just <c>trimStrings</c>.
039 *
040 * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
041 *    <li class='warn'>This class is not thread safe.
042 *    <li>Whitespace and JSON comments are tolerated &mdash; comments are silently skipped to mirror
043 *       Juneau's existing JSON parser behavior.
044 *    <li>The cursor is purely structural; object swaps and {@code @Schema} annotations have no
045 *       effect.  Use the POJO databind path on {@link JsonParser} for those.
046 * </ul>
047 */
048@SuppressWarnings({
049   "java:S3776", // Cognitive complexity acceptable for JSON tokenizer state machine
050   "java:S6541", // Brain method acceptable for tokenizer dispatch
051   "java:S115",  // Message-key constant uses UPPER_camelCase convention.
052   "resource"    // The cursor's underlying ParserPipe is owned by the caller via try-with-resources on the cursor itself; Eclipse JDT flags the inner pipe as unclosed but that's by design.
053})
054public class JsonTokenReader implements TokenReader {
055
056   /**
057    * Cursor-level settings honored by {@link JsonTokenReader}.
058    *
059    * <p>
060    * Pipe-level concerns (auto-close, unbuffered, charsets) are configured on the
061    * {@link ParserPipe} itself before construction; only behaviors that the cursor applies to
062    * already-decoded tokens live here.
063    *
064    * @param trimStrings If <jk>true</jk>, leading/trailing whitespace is trimmed from
065    *    {@link TokenType#VALUE_STRING} and {@link TokenType#FIELD_NAME} values returned by the
066    *    cursor.
067    */
068   public record Settings(boolean trimStrings) {
069
070      /** Default canonical setting: no string trimming. */
071      public static final Settings DEFAULT = new Settings(false);
072   }
073
074   // ==============================================================================================
075   // Subclass extension surface.
076   //
077   // The fields and methods below are part of JsonTokenReader's documented subclass-extension
078   // contract.  Cross-package subclasses (Json5TokenReader in marshall.json5, JsonlTokenReader in
079   // marshall.jsonl) need access to the cursor's internal state machine and per-token scratch
080   // buffers in order to implement format dialects (single-quoted strings, bare identifiers,
081   // trailing commas, missing values, etc.).
082   //
083   // Fields are protected (rather than private + accessor) because the dispatch methods
084   // (readValueStart, readFieldNameOrEndObject, readCommaOrEndArray, ...) directly mutate the
085   // per-token scratch and the state machine variable.  Wrapping them in get/set pairs would add
086   // indirection without improving safety.  In return, subclasses MUST treat them as
087   // implementation-coupled: Juneau reserves the right to evolve them across major versions.
088   // ==============================================================================================
089
090   /**
091    * Error-message format string for invalid JSON numbers.  Used with
092    * {@link #parseException(String, Object...)}.
093    */
094   protected static final String MSG_invalidNumber = "Invalid JSON number: ''{0}''";
095
096   /**
097    * Error-message format string for unexpected characters at the cursor's current position.
098    * Used with {@link #parseException(String, Object...)}.
099    */
100   protected static final String MSG_unexpectedChar = "Unexpected character: ''{0}''";
101
102   /**
103    * State-machine state: at root, after <c>[</c>, after <c>:</c>, or after <c>,</c> inside an
104    * array.  The next read should be a JSON value (object, array, or scalar).
105    */
106   protected static final int S00_expectValue = 0;
107
108   /**
109    * State-machine state: after <c>{</c> or after <c>,</c> inside an object.  The next read
110    * should be a field name (string).
111    */
112   protected static final int S01_expectFieldName = 1;
113
114   /**
115    * State-machine state: just emitted a {@link TokenType#FIELD_NAME}.  The next char should be
116    * <c>:</c>.
117    */
118   protected static final int S02_expectColon = 2;
119
120   /**
121    * State-machine state: just emitted a value inside an object.  The next char should be
122    * <c>,</c> (followed by another field name) or <c>}</c>.
123    */
124   protected static final int S03_expectCommaOrEndObject = 3;
125
126   /**
127    * State-machine state: just emitted a value inside an array.  The next char should be
128    * <c>,</c> (followed by another value) or <c>]</c>.
129    */
130   protected static final int S04_expectCommaOrEndArray = 4;
131
132   /**
133    * State-machine state: the root document has been fully consumed.  The next call to
134    * {@link #next()} returns {@link TokenType#END_OF_STREAM}.
135    */
136   protected static final int S05_end = 5;
137
138   /** Underlying character-level reader; subclasses use this for direct {@code read}/{@code unread}/{@code mark}/{@code getMarked} calls during dialect-specific tokenization. */
139   protected final ParserReader r;
140
141   private final ParserPipe pipe;
142
143   /** Cursor-level settings (currently just <c>trimStrings</c>). */
144   protected final Settings settings;
145
146   private final JsonParserSession session;
147
148   /** The token most recently emitted by {@link #next()}.  Subclasses set this when overriding state-machine dispatch methods. */
149   protected TokenType currentToken = TokenType.NOT_AVAILABLE;
150
151   /** Current state-machine state; one of the {@code STATE_*} constants. */
152   protected int state = S00_expectValue;
153
154   // Container stack: 0 bits = object, 1 bits = array.  Limited to 64 levels of nesting
155   // (tracked via {@link #depth}); deeper documents fall back to a heap-allocated overflow array.
156   private long containerBits;
157   private boolean[] containerOverflow;
158
159   /** Current container nesting depth (0 = root). */
160   protected int depth;
161
162   /** Field name buffer for the current {@link TokenType#FIELD_NAME} token. */
163   protected String currentFieldName;
164
165   /** String value buffer for the current {@link TokenType#VALUE_STRING} token. */
166   protected String currentString;
167
168   /** Numeric lexeme buffer for the current {@link TokenType#VALUE_NUMBER} token (preserved verbatim from the input). */
169   protected String currentNumberLexeme;
170
171   private Number currentNumber;
172
173   /** Boolean value buffer for the current {@link TokenType#VALUE_BOOLEAN} token. */
174   protected boolean currentBoolean;
175
176   /**
177    * Constructor with default {@link Settings}.
178    *
179    * @param pipe The parser input pipe to read from.  Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
180    * @throws IOException If a problem occurred opening the underlying reader.
181    */
182   public JsonTokenReader(ParserPipe pipe) throws IOException {
183      this(pipe, Settings.DEFAULT, null);
184   }
185
186   /**
187    * Constructor with explicit {@link Settings}.
188    *
189    * @param pipe The parser input pipe to read from.  Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
190    * @param settings The cursor-level settings.  Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
191    * @throws IOException If a problem occurred opening the underlying reader.
192    */
193   public JsonTokenReader(ParserPipe pipe, Settings settings) throws IOException {
194      this(pipe, settings, null);
195   }
196
197   /**
198    * Constructor used by {@link JsonParser#parseTokens(Object)} to plumb the calling parser's
199    * session in so that {@link #read(Class)} can delegate to the existing databind path.
200    *
201    * @param pipe The parser input pipe to read from.  Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
202    * @param settings The cursor-level settings.  Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
203    * @param session The {@link JsonParserSession} to use for {@link #read(Class)} delegation,
204    *    or <jk>null</jk> to disable {@code read} on this reader (it will throw
205    *    {@link UnsupportedOperationException}).
206    * @throws IOException If a problem occurred opening the underlying reader.
207    */
208   public JsonTokenReader(ParserPipe pipe, Settings settings, JsonParserSession session) throws IOException {
209      this.pipe = pipe;
210      this.r = pipe.getParserReader();
211      this.settings = settings;
212      this.session = session;
213   }
214
215   // ==============================================================================================
216   // State-machine summary.  next() reads the next non-whitespace, non-comment character from the
217   // underlying ParserReader and dispatches to one of the readXxx() methods below based on the
218   // cursor's current state.  Each readXxx() method is overridable by subclasses to add
219   // dialect-specific behavior.
220   //
221   //   S00_expectValue                 - At root, after [, after :, after , inside [.
222   //                                        Expecting a JSON value: { [ " digit t f n.
223   //                                        Dispatches to readValueStart.
224   //   S01_expectFieldName             - After { or after , inside {.
225   //                                        Expecting a quoted string field name (or }).
226   //                                        Dispatches to readFieldNameOrEndObject.
227   //   S02_expectColon                 - Just emitted a FIELD_NAME.  Expecting :.
228   //                                        Handled inline by readColonThenValue.
229   //   S03_expectCommaOrEndObject      - Just emitted a value inside {.  Expecting , or }.
230   //                                        Dispatches to readCommaOrEndObject.
231   //   S04_expectCommaOrEndArray       - Just emitted a value inside [.  Expecting , or ].
232   //                                        Dispatches to readCommaOrEndArray.
233   //   S05_end                         - Root document fully consumed.  next() returns
234   //                                        END_OF_STREAM.
235   //
236   // State transitions are performed by afterValue() (sets the post-value state based on the
237   // enclosing container) and by the dispatch methods themselves.
238   // ==============================================================================================
239   @Override /* Overridden from TokenReader */
240   public TokenType next() throws IOException, ParseException {
241      if (state == S05_end) {
242         currentToken = TokenType.END_OF_STREAM;
243         return currentToken;
244      }
245
246      // Reset per-token scratch.
247      currentFieldName = null;
248      currentString = null;
249      currentNumberLexeme = null;
250      currentNumber = null;
251
252      int c = readSkipWsAndComments();
253      if (c == -1) {
254         if (state != S00_expectValue || depth != 0)
255            throw parseException("Unexpected end of input");
256         state = S05_end;
257         currentToken = TokenType.END_OF_STREAM;
258         return currentToken;
259      }
260
261      switch (state) {
262         case S00_expectValue                 -> readValueStart(c);
263         case S01_expectFieldName             -> readFieldNameOrEndObject(c);
264         case S02_expectColon                 -> readColonThenValue(c);
265         case S03_expectCommaOrEndObject      -> readCommaOrEndObject(c);
266         case S04_expectCommaOrEndArray       -> readCommaOrEndArray(c);
267         default                              -> throw parseException("Internal tokenizer state error");
268      }
269      return currentToken;
270   }
271
272   @Override /* Overridden from TokenReader */
273   public TokenType getCurrentToken() {
274      return currentToken;
275   }
276
277   @Override /* Overridden from TokenReader */
278   public int getDepth() {
279      return depth;
280   }
281
282   @Override /* Overridden from TokenReader */
283   public String getFieldName() {
284      if (currentToken != TokenType.FIELD_NAME)
285         throw new IllegalStateException("Current token is not FIELD_NAME (was " + currentToken + ")");
286      return currentFieldName;
287   }
288
289   @Override /* Overridden from TokenReader */
290   public String getString() {
291      switch (currentToken) {
292         case VALUE_STRING:    return currentString;
293         case FIELD_NAME:      return currentFieldName;
294         case VALUE_NUMBER:    return currentNumberLexeme;
295         case VALUE_BOOLEAN:   return Boolean.toString(currentBoolean);
296         case VALUE_NULL:      return null;
297         default: throw new IllegalStateException(
298            "Current token does not have a string view (was " + currentToken + ")");
299      }
300   }
301
302   @Override /* Overridden from TokenReader */
303   public Number getNumber() throws ParseException {
304      if (currentToken != TokenType.VALUE_NUMBER)
305         throw new IllegalStateException(
306            "Current token is not VALUE_NUMBER (was " + currentToken + ")");
307      if (currentNumber == null)
308         currentNumber = StringUtils.parseNumber(currentNumberLexeme, null);
309      return currentNumber;
310   }
311
312   @Override /* Overridden from TokenReader */
313   public String getNumberLexeme() {
314      if (currentToken != TokenType.VALUE_NUMBER)
315         throw new IllegalStateException(
316            "Current token is not VALUE_NUMBER (was " + currentToken + ")");
317      return currentNumberLexeme;
318   }
319
320   @Override /* Overridden from TokenReader */
321   public boolean getBool() {
322      if (currentToken != TokenType.VALUE_BOOLEAN)
323         throw new IllegalStateException(
324            "Current token is not VALUE_BOOLEAN (was " + currentToken + ")");
325      return currentBoolean;
326   }
327
328   @Override /* Overridden from TokenReader */
329   public byte[] getBinary() {
330      throw new IllegalStateException("JSON does not produce VALUE_BINARY tokens");
331   }
332
333   @Override /* Overridden from TokenReader */
334   public boolean canRead() throws IOException, ParseException {
335      // Auto-advance through structural separators that don't themselves emit value tokens.
336      // This lets users write `while (r.canRead()) bean = r.read(...)` for array
337      // streaming without manually consuming commas.
338      advanceToValueState();
339      if (state != S00_expectValue)
340         return false;
341      // At S00_expectValue the next non-whitespace char might still be a closer (empty
342      // container).  Peek to disambiguate.
343      var c = readSkipWsAndComments();
344      if (c == -1)
345         return false;
346      r.unread();
347      return c != ']' && c != '}';
348   }
349
350   /**
351    * Helper for {@link #canRead()} and {@link #read(ClassMeta)}: if the cursor is
352    * sitting on a structural separator (comma between array elements, colon after a field name),
353    * consume it so the cursor lands at S00_expectValue.  No-op if already at a value
354    * boundary or at end-of-container.
355    */
356   private void advanceToValueState() throws IOException, ParseException {
357      while (true) {
358         switch (state) {
359            case S04_expectCommaOrEndArray: {
360               var c = readSkipWsAndComments();
361               if (c == -1 || c == ']') {
362                  if (c != -1) r.unread();
363                  return;
364               }
365               if (c != ',')
366                  throw parseException("Expected ',' or ']' but got ''{0}''", (char) c);
367               // If a trailing-comma case follows (next non-ws is ']'), keep the comma-or-end
368               // state so a subsequent next() closes the container correctly.  Format-specific
369               // hook: plain JSON rejects trailing commas, JSON5 accepts.
370               var c2 = readSkipWsAndComments();
371               if (c2 == ']') {
372                  r.unread();
373                  if (!allowsTrailingComma())
374                     throw parseException("Trailing comma not allowed in this format");
375                  return;  // state stays S04_expectCommaOrEndArray
376               }
377               if (c2 != -1) r.unread();
378               state = S00_expectValue;
379               break;
380            }
381            case S03_expectCommaOrEndObject: {
382               var c = readSkipWsAndComments();
383               if (c == -1 || c == '}') {
384                  if (c != -1) r.unread();
385                  return;
386               }
387               if (c != ',')
388                  throw parseException("Expected ',' or '}' but got ''{0}''", (char) c);
389               var c2 = readSkipWsAndComments();
390               if (c2 == '}') {
391                  r.unread();
392                  if (!allowsTrailingComma())
393                     throw parseException("Trailing comma not allowed in this format");
394                  return;  // state stays S03_expectCommaOrEndObject
395               }
396               if (c2 != -1) r.unread();
397               // After a comma inside an object, the next thing is a field name, not a value.
398               state = S01_expectFieldName;
399               return;
400            }
401            case S02_expectColon: {
402               var c = readSkipWsAndComments();
403               if (c == -1)
404                  return;
405               if (c != ':')
406                  throw parseException("Expected ':' after field name but got ''{0}''", (char) c);
407               state = S00_expectValue;
408               break;
409            }
410            default:
411               return;
412         }
413      }
414   }
415
416   /**
417    * Subclass extension point: returns whether trailing commas are accepted before <c>]</c> /
418    * <c>}</c>.
419    *
420    * <p>
421    * Default returns <jk>false</jk> (strict RFC-8259 JSON).  Json5TokenReader overrides this to
422    * return <jk>true</jk> for the JSON5 dialect.
423    *
424    * @return <jk>true</jk> if a trailing comma should be silently accepted; <jk>false</jk> if it
425    *    should produce a parse error.
426    */
427   protected boolean allowsTrailingComma() {
428      return false;
429   }
430
431   @Override /* Overridden from TokenReader */
432   public <T> T read(Class<T> type) throws IOException, ParseException {
433      return read(session().getClassMeta(type));
434   }
435
436   @Override /* Overridden from TokenReader */
437   public <T> T read(ClassMeta<T> type) throws IOException, ParseException {
438      var s = session();
439      advanceToValueState();
440      assertReadValueState();
441      try {
442         T o = s.parseAnything(type, r, null, null);
443         markValueConsumed();
444         return o;
445      } catch (Exception e) {
446         if (e instanceof IOException ioe) throw ioe;
447         if (e instanceof ParseException pe) throw pe;
448         throw new ParseException(s, "read failed: {0}", e.getMessage());
449      }
450   }
451
452   @Override /* Overridden from TokenReader */
453   public <T> T read(Type type, Type... args) throws IOException, ParseException {
454      return read(session().<T>getClassMeta(type, args));
455   }
456
457   private JsonParserSession session() {
458      if (session == null)
459         throw new UnsupportedOperationException(
460            "read is not available on this JsonTokenReader (no session attached). " +
461            "Open the cursor via JsonParser.parseTokens(...) to enable read.");
462      return session;
463   }
464
465   /**
466    * Validates that the cursor is positioned at a value boundary (i.e. the next emitted token
467    * would be value-shaped).
468    */
469   private void assertReadValueState() {
470      if (state != S00_expectValue)
471         throw new IllegalStateException(MSG_READ_NON_VALUE_STATE);
472   }
473
474   /**
475    * Resyncs the cursor's state machine after {@code parseAnything} consumed a value.  The cursor's
476    * depth does not change (the value was contained at the current depth); only the post-value
477    * separator-expectation state needs to update.
478    */
479   private void markValueConsumed() {
480      // We did not surface an explicit token to the caller, but functionally this is "a value
481      // just got emitted at the current depth," so the same post-value transition that next()
482      // would have performed applies.
483      afterValue();
484      // The current token slot is meaningless after read (the caller has the bound POJO).
485      // Set a sentinel so accidental getStringValue() / getCurrentToken() usage doesn't return
486      // a stale prior token's value.
487      currentToken = TokenType.NOT_AVAILABLE;
488   }
489
490   @Override /* Overridden from TokenReader */
491   public void skipChildren() throws IOException, ParseException {
492      if (currentToken != TokenType.START_OBJECT && currentToken != TokenType.START_ARRAY)
493         return;
494      var startDepth = depth - 1;
495      while (depth > startDepth) {
496         var t = next();
497         if (t == TokenType.END_OF_STREAM)
498            throw parseException("Unexpected end of input while skipping children");
499      }
500   }
501
502   @Override /* Overridden from TokenReader */
503   public void close() throws IOException {
504      pipe.close();
505   }
506
507   // State-machine dispatch
508
509   /**
510    * State-machine dispatch: handles the next character at {@link #S00_expectValue}.
511    *
512    * <p>
513    * Subclasses (e.g. Json5TokenReader) override this to add dialect-specific value shapes
514    * (single-quoted strings, bare identifiers, missing-value handling).
515    *
516    * @param c The next non-whitespace, non-comment character read from the input.
517    * @throws IOException If a problem occurred reading from the underlying stream.
518    * @throws ParseException If the input is malformed at this position.
519    */
520   protected void readValueStart(int c) throws IOException, ParseException {
521      switch (c) {
522         case '{':
523            pushContainer(false);
524            currentToken = TokenType.START_OBJECT;
525            state = S01_expectFieldName;
526            return;
527         case '[':
528            pushContainer(true);
529            currentToken = TokenType.START_ARRAY;
530            state = S00_expectValue;
531            return;
532         case '"':
533            r.unread();
534            currentString = maybeTrim(readString());
535            currentToken = TokenType.VALUE_STRING;
536            afterValue();
537            return;
538         case 't', 'f', 'n':
539            r.unread();
540            readKeywordValue(c);
541            afterValue();
542            return;
543         case ']':
544            if (depth > 0 && isInArray()) {
545               popContainer();
546               currentToken = TokenType.END_ARRAY;
547               afterValue();
548               return;
549            }
550            throw parseException(MSG_unexpectedChar, (char) c);
551         default:
552            if (isNumberStart(c)) {
553               r.unread();
554               readNumberValue();
555               afterValue();
556               return;
557            }
558            throw parseException(MSG_unexpectedChar, (char) c);
559      }
560   }
561
562   /**
563    * State-machine dispatch: handles the next character at {@link #S01_expectFieldName}.
564    *
565    * <p>
566    * Subclasses (e.g. Json5TokenReader) override this to accept bare-identifier field names and
567    * single-quoted field names.
568    *
569    * @param c The next non-whitespace character read from the input.
570    * @throws IOException If a problem occurred reading from the underlying stream.
571    * @throws ParseException If the input is malformed at this position.
572    */
573   protected void readFieldNameOrEndObject(int c) throws IOException, ParseException {
574      if (c == '}') {
575         popContainer();
576         currentToken = TokenType.END_OBJECT;
577         afterValue();
578         return;
579      }
580      if (c != '"')
581         throw parseException("Expected '\"' for field name but got ''{0}''", (char) c);
582      r.unread();
583      currentFieldName = maybeTrim(readString());
584      currentToken = TokenType.FIELD_NAME;
585      state = S02_expectColon;
586   }
587
588   private String maybeTrim(String s) {
589      if (!settings.trimStrings || s == null)
590         return s;
591      return s.trim();
592   }
593
594   private void readColonThenValue(int c) throws IOException, ParseException {
595      if (c != ':')
596         throw parseException("Expected ':' after field name but got ''{0}''", (char) c);
597      var c2 = readSkipWsAndComments();
598      if (c2 == -1)
599         throw parseException("Unexpected end of input after ':'");
600      state = S00_expectValue;
601      readValueStart(c2);
602   }
603
604   /**
605    * State-machine dispatch: handles the next character at
606    * {@link #S03_expectCommaOrEndObject}.
607    *
608    * <p>
609    * Subclasses (e.g. Json5TokenReader) override this to accept trailing commas before <c>}</c>.
610    *
611    * @param c The next non-whitespace character read from the input.
612    * @throws IOException If a problem occurred reading from the underlying stream.
613    * @throws ParseException If the input is malformed at this position.
614    */
615   protected void readCommaOrEndObject(int c) throws IOException, ParseException {
616      if (c == '}') {
617         popContainer();
618         currentToken = TokenType.END_OBJECT;
619         afterValue();
620         return;
621      }
622      if (c != ',')
623         throw parseException("Expected ',' or '}' but got ''{0}''", (char) c);
624      var c2 = readSkipWsAndComments();
625      if (c2 == -1)
626         throw parseException("Unexpected end of input after ','");
627      // After a comma inside an object the next thing must be a field name (or '}' is illegal here).
628      state = S01_expectFieldName;
629      readFieldNameOrEndObject(c2);
630   }
631
632   /**
633    * State-machine dispatch: handles the next character at
634    * {@link #S04_expectCommaOrEndArray}.
635    *
636    * <p>
637    * Subclasses (e.g. Json5TokenReader) override this to accept trailing commas before <c>]</c>.
638    *
639    * @param c The next non-whitespace character read from the input.
640    * @throws IOException If a problem occurred reading from the underlying stream.
641    * @throws ParseException If the input is malformed at this position.
642    */
643   protected void readCommaOrEndArray(int c) throws IOException, ParseException {
644      if (c == ']') {
645         popContainer();
646         currentToken = TokenType.END_ARRAY;
647         afterValue();
648         return;
649      }
650      if (c != ',')
651         throw parseException("Expected ',' or ']' but got ''{0}''", (char) c);
652      var c2 = readSkipWsAndComments();
653      if (c2 == -1)
654         throw parseException("Unexpected end of input after ','");
655      state = S00_expectValue;
656      readValueStart(c2);
657   }
658
659   /**
660    * Sets the post-value state based on the enclosing container.
661    *
662    * <p>
663    * Subclasses (e.g. JsonlTokenReader) override this when the format treats consecutive
664    * top-level values differently &mdash; JSONL transitions back to {@link #S00_expectValue}
665    * at depth 0 instead of {@link #S05_end} so the next line is read.
666    */
667   protected void afterValue() {
668      if (depth == 0)
669         state = S05_end;
670      else if (isInArray())
671         state = S04_expectCommaOrEndArray;
672      else
673         state = S03_expectCommaOrEndObject;
674   }
675
676   // Scalar readers
677
678   /**
679    * Reads a double-quoted JSON string from the current input position.
680    *
681    * <p>
682    * Handles escape sequences (<c>\\n</c>, <c>\\t</c>, <c>\\u<i>NNNN</i></c>, etc.) inline via
683    * {@link ParserReader#replace(char)}.  Subclasses (e.g. Json5TokenReader) override this to
684    * also accept single-quoted strings.
685    *
686    * @return The parsed string (without surrounding quotes).
687    * @throws IOException If a problem occurred reading from the underlying stream.
688    * @throws ParseException If the input is malformed at this position.
689    */
690   protected String readString() throws IOException, ParseException {
691      r.mark();
692      var qc = r.read();
693      if (qc != '"')
694         throw parseException("Did not find quote character marking beginning of string");
695      String s = null;
696      var inEscape = false;
697      var c = 0;
698      while (c != -1) {
699         c = r.read();
700         if (c <= 0x1F && c != -1)
701            throw parseException("Unescaped control character encountered: ''0x{0}''", String.format("%04X", c));
702         if (inEscape) {
703            switch (c) {
704               case 'n': r.replace('\n'); break;
705               case 'r': r.replace('\r'); break;
706               case 't': r.replace('\t'); break;
707               case 'f': r.replace('\f'); break;
708               case 'b': r.replace('\b'); break;
709               case '\\': r.replace('\\'); break;
710               case '/': r.replace('/'); break;
711               case '\'': r.replace('\''); break;
712               case '"': r.replace('"'); break;
713               case 'u': {
714                  var hex = r.read(4);
715                  try {
716                     r.replace(Integer.parseInt(hex, 16), 6);
717                  } catch (@SuppressWarnings("unused") NumberFormatException e) {
718                     throw parseException("Invalid Unicode escape sequence in string");
719                  }
720                  break;
721               }
722               default:
723                  throw parseException("Invalid escape sequence in string");
724            }
725            inEscape = false;
726         } else {
727            if (c == '\\') {
728               inEscape = true;
729               r.delete();
730            } else if (c == qc) {
731               s = r.getMarked(1, -1);
732               break;
733            }
734         }
735      }
736      if (s == null)
737         throw parseException("Unterminated string");
738      return s;
739   }
740
741   /**
742    * Reads one of the JSON keyword literals (<c>true</c>, <c>false</c>, <c>null</c>) starting
743    * with the given lookahead character.  Sets {@link #currentToken} and {@link #currentBoolean}
744    * accordingly.
745    *
746    * @param c The first character of the keyword (one of <c>'t'</c>, <c>'f'</c>, <c>'n'</c>).
747    * @throws IOException If a problem occurred reading from the underlying stream.
748    * @throws ParseException If the keyword is malformed.
749    */
750   protected void readKeywordValue(int c) throws IOException, ParseException {
751      switch (c) {
752         case 't':
753            expectLiteral("true");
754            currentBoolean = true;
755            currentToken = TokenType.VALUE_BOOLEAN;
756            return;
757         case 'f':
758            expectLiteral("false");
759            currentBoolean = false;
760            currentToken = TokenType.VALUE_BOOLEAN;
761            return;
762         case 'n':
763            expectLiteral("null");
764            currentToken = TokenType.VALUE_NULL;
765            return;
766         default:
767            throw parseException(MSG_unexpectedChar, (char) c);
768      }
769   }
770
771   private void expectLiteral(String literal) throws IOException, ParseException {
772      var s = r.read(literal.length());
773      if (!literal.equals(s))
774         throw parseException("Unrecognized syntax.  Expected=''{0}'', Actual=''{1}''", literal, s);
775   }
776
777   /**
778    * Reads a JSON number from the current input position.  Captures the lexeme via
779    * {@link ParserReader#parseNumberString()} and validates it; defers actual numeric parsing
780    * to {@link #getNumber()} (lazy).  Sets {@link #currentNumberLexeme} and {@link #currentToken}.
781    *
782    * @throws IOException If a problem occurred reading from the underlying stream.
783    * @throws ParseException If the input is not a valid JSON number.
784    */
785   protected void readNumberValue() throws IOException, ParseException {
786      // Mirror JsonParserSession#parseNumber's lexeme-collection: collect "number chars" then
787      // hand off to StringUtils.parseNumber.  The lexeme is preserved as currentNumberLexeme so
788      // precision-sensitive consumers can recover the original textual form (resolved Q6).
789      var s = r.parseNumberString();
790      validateNumberLexeme(s);
791      currentNumberLexeme = s;
792      currentToken = TokenType.VALUE_NUMBER;
793      // Number itself is parsed lazily on first getNumberValue() call.
794   }
795
796   private void validateNumberLexeme(String s) throws ParseException {
797      if (s.isEmpty())
798         throw parseException(MSG_invalidNumber, s);
799      var isNegative = false;
800      var c = s.charAt(0);
801      if (c == '-') {
802         isNegative = true;
803         c = (s.length() == 1 ? 'x' : s.charAt(1));
804      }
805      if (c == '.')
806         throw parseException(MSG_invalidNumber, s);
807      if (c == '0' && s.length() > (isNegative ? 2 : 1)) {
808         var c2 = s.charAt(isNegative ? 2 : 1);
809         if (c2 != '.' && c2 != 'e' && c2 != 'E')
810            throw parseException(MSG_invalidNumber, s);
811      }
812      var i = s.indexOf('.');
813      if (i != -1 && (s.length() == (i + 1) || !isDigit(s.charAt(i + 1))))
814         throw parseException(MSG_invalidNumber, s);
815   }
816
817   // Reader helpers
818
819   /**
820    * Reads the next non-whitespace, non-comment character or -1 at end of stream.
821    *
822    * @return The next significant character, or <c>-1</c> at end of input.
823    * @throws IOException If a problem occurred reading from the underlying stream.
824    * @throws ParseException If a JSON comment is malformed.
825    */
826   protected int readSkipWsAndComments() throws IOException, ParseException {
827      while (true) {
828         var c = r.read();
829         if (c == -1)
830            return -1;
831         if (c == '/')
832            skipComment();
833         else if (!Character.isWhitespace(c))
834            return c;
835      }
836   }
837
838   @SuppressWarnings({
839      "java:S2677" // read() return intentionally discarded: consuming the already-peeked '/' that closes a block comment.
840   })
841   private void skipComment() throws IOException, ParseException {
842      var c = r.read();
843      if (c == '*') {
844         while (true) {
845            c = r.read();
846            if (c == -1)
847               throw parseException("Unterminated block comment");
848            if (c == '*' && r.peek() == '/') {
849               r.read();
850               return;
851            }
852         }
853      }
854      if (c == '/') {
855         while (true) {
856            c = r.read();
857            if (c == -1 || c == '\n')
858               return;
859         }
860      }
861      throw parseException("Open ended comment");
862   }
863
864   /**
865    * Returns whether the given character could start a JSON number lexeme (digit or minus sign).
866    *
867    * @param c The character.
868    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the character could start a number.
869    */
870   protected static boolean isNumberStart(int c) {
871      return c == '-' || (c >= '0' && c <= '9');
872   }
873
874   /**
875    * Returns whether the given character is an ASCII decimal digit.
876    *
877    * @param c The character.
878    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the character is between <c>'0'</c> and <c>'9'</c> inclusive.
879    */
880   protected static boolean isDigit(int c) {
881      return c >= '0' && c <= '9';
882   }
883
884   // Container stack
885
886   /**
887    * Pushes a new container onto the cursor's depth stack.
888    *
889    * @param isArray <jk>true</jk> if the new container is an array; <jk>false</jk> for an object.
890    */
891   protected void pushContainer(boolean isArray) {
892      if (depth < 64) {
893         if (isArray)
894            containerBits |= (1L << depth);
895         else
896            containerBits &= ~(1L << depth);
897      } else {
898         if (containerOverflow == null || depth - 64 >= containerOverflow.length)
899            growOverflow();
900         containerOverflow[depth - 64] = isArray;
901      }
902      depth++;
903   }
904
905   /**
906    * Pops the top container off the cursor's depth stack.
907    *
908    * @throws ParseException If the stack is already empty (defensive only; well-formed JSON
909    *    cannot trigger this).
910    */
911   protected void popContainer() throws ParseException {
912      if (depth == 0)
913         throw parseException("Container stack underflow");
914      depth--;
915   }
916
917   /**
918    * Returns whether the cursor's current container (the one most recently pushed) is an array.
919    *
920    * @return <jk>true</jk> if currently inside an array; <jk>false</jk> if inside an object.
921    */
922   protected boolean isInArray() {
923      var top = depth - 1;
924      if (top < 64)
925         return (containerBits & (1L << top)) != 0;
926      return containerOverflow[top - 64];
927   }
928
929   private void growOverflow() {
930      var n = containerOverflow == null ? 16 : containerOverflow.length << 1;
931      var bigger = new boolean[n];
932      if (containerOverflow != null)
933         System.arraycopy(containerOverflow, 0, bigger, 0, containerOverflow.length);
934      containerOverflow = bigger;
935   }
936
937   /**
938    * Builds a {@link ParseException} with the given message + {@link java.text.MessageFormat}-style
939    * arguments.  Used by all dispatch methods to surface input-malformed errors with consistent
940    * formatting.
941    *
942    * @param message The exception message format string ({0}, {1}, ...).
943    * @param args The format arguments.
944    * @return A new {@link ParseException} ready to throw.
945    */
946   protected ParseException parseException(String message, Object... args) {
947      return new ParseException(message, args);
948   }
949}
